Medium acidity

Domaine Jean-Paul ThevenetBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still White

Beaujolais Nouveau

3.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Festive · Easygoing

Maison Georges DuboeufBeaujolais€€€€€€Still Red

Duboeuf's Beaujolais Nouveau is the widely-distributed reference for the third-Thursday-November release: carbonic-maceration Gamay bottled and released on the third Thursday of November, with characteristically exuberant fruit and soft structure.

Cuvée page →

Beaujolais-Villages

3.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Domaine des NuguesBeaujolais€€€€€€Still Red

Domaine des Nugues' estate Beaujolais-Villages from the Lancié village in the northern Villages zone: clean, accessible Gamay with strawberry-cherry freshness.

Cuvée page →

Brouilly

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Château de la ChaizeBeaujolais€€€€€€Still Red

Château de la Chaize's estate Brouilly from its historic 220-hectare Odenas property: full-flavoured, traditionally structured Gamay with ripe red fruit and reliable house style.

Cuvée page →

Brouilly Cuvée Réservée

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Rustic · Contemplative

Château de la ChaizeBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Château de la Chaize's mid-tier Brouilly, longer-aged and more structured than the classique, with ripe fruit concentration and earthy Brouilly depth.

Cuvée page →

Brouilly L'Espigon

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Elegant

Château de la ChaizeBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Post-2017 renovation cuvée from the L'Espigon parcel: richer and more oak-influenced than the classique, with full body and concentrated dark-fruit structure.

Cuvée page →

Roussette

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Domaine Terres Dorées (Jean-Paul Brun)Beaujolais€€€€€€€Still White

Named for the golden-limestone colour of the Pierres Dorées hillsides, Roussette is Terres Dorées' richest white: beeswax, ripe peach and hazelnut character from old-barrel Chardonnay on the golden stone terroir.

Cuvée page →

Aria du Chateau de la Riviere

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Bold

Chateau de la RiviereBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

The prestige selection of Chateau de la Riviere, sourced exclusively from the estate's oldest Merlot vines in a single 1.5-hectare plot and aged entirely in new French oak. Produced only in the best vintages; a luxury expression of Fronsac terroir.

Cuvée page →

Aromes de Pavie

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Savoury

Chateau PavieBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

Originally the second wine of Pavie, now an independent brand since 2018 sourced from a dedicated 11-hectare east-plateau parcel. Merlot-Cabernet Franc blend of medium body with dark cherry, espresso and salty-mineral character; approachable on release.

Cuvée page →

Bauduc Les Trois Hectares Blanc

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Chateau BauducBordeaux€€€€€€Still White

Single-plot Semillon from 75-year-old vines in a three-hectare block; the house premium white. Dry and elegant with the waxy, textured Semillon character that recalls white Pessac-Leognan at a fraction of the price. Anne Krebiehl MW-praised.

Cuvée page →

Bauduc Les Trois Hectares Rouge

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau BauducBordeaux€€€€€€Still Red

Bauduc's premium red from a single Bordeaux Superieur block: 75% Merlot, 25% Cabernet Sauvignon aged in small barrels for added structure. Anne Krebiehl MW describes it as juicy, plush, and beautifully open with oak-kissed fruit.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Angelus

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Elegant

Chateau AngelusBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Merlot and Cabernet Franc from 27 classified hectares at the foot of Saint-Emilion's slope. Generous, opulent and structured, with dark cherry, violets and cedar complexity; certified organic from the 2021 vintage.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Bauduc Bordeaux Superieur

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Fruity

Chateau BauducBordeaux€€€€€€Still Red

Bauduc's entry-level claret; a straightforward Merlot-Cabernet blend offering reliable, juicy red-fruit character and soft tannins at an accessible price. Drink now or hold for up to seven years. The wine is consistently good for its level.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Beau-Sejour Becot

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Chateau Beau-Sejour BecotBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still Red

From 16.5 hectares of ancient underground cellars carved into the limestone of Saint-Emilion's plateau, Beau-Sejour Becot offers silk and velvet textures with floral aromatics, dark fruits and a crushed-stone mineral lift. Recent vintages show increasing use of amphora for freshness.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Canon

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Chateau CanonBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Premier Grand Cru Classe B from the Saint-Emilion limestone plateau. Owned by the Wertheimer family (Chanel), Canon produces Merlot-dominant blends of great classical finesse and ageing capacity, with cedar, mint and graphite precision.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Cheval Blanc

5.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Chateau Cheval BlancBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Cabernet Franc-dominant Saint-Emilion of breathtaking silkiness: violets and graphite on the nose, an opulent yet precise palate of black cherry and tobacco, seamless tannin structure and a finish of uncommon length.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Haut-Brion

5.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Chateau Haut-BrionBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Merlot-led first-growth Pessac-Leognan red combining cassis and graphite with earthy tobacco and cedar. Velvety tannins and exceptional length set it apart from Medoc peers.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Haut-Lariveau

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Fruity

Chateau Moulin Pey-LabrieBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

100% Merlot Fronsac from the 4.5-hectare Haut-Lariveau parcel acquired by the Hubau family in 1990. More approachable and fruit-forward than its Canon-Fronsac sibling, farmed with the same biodynamic discipline.

Cuvée page →

Chateau La Mission Haut-Brion

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Chateau La Mission Haut-BrionBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

La Mission Haut-Brion combines the gravel terroir of Talence with intense, brooding dark fruit. Blackberry, scorched earth, and graphite underpin a structured, powerful palate with velvety tannins and a finish that rivals the great first growths.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Pape Clement

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Chateau Pape ClementBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

From the oldest wine estate in Bordeaux, tracing its history to the 14th-century papacy. Cabernet-and-Merlot blend delivering cassis, graphite, and tobacco with flawless tannins; in great vintages approaches first-growth complexity.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Pavie

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Chateau PavieBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

From 42 hectares at Saint-Emilion's highest point (110m), Pavie delivers concentrated, structured Merlot-dominant blends with extraordinary mineral depth. Pairs elegance with considerable power; requires minimum 15 years to fully integrate.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Roc de Cambes

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Savoury

Chateau Roc de CambesBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Francois Mitjavile's Cotes de Bourg cult wine, modelled on the exacting principles of his Saint-Emilion estate Tertre-Roteboeuf: very late harvesting, low yields, and 100% new oak since 1996. Pushed the Cotes de Bourg to its quality ceiling.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Smith Haut Lafitte Rouge

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Chateau Smith Haut LafitteBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still Red

A benchmark Pessac-Leognan Cabernet since the Cathiard family's acquisition in 1990. Cassis, graphite, and tobacco define a precise, structured palate; 60% new oak ageing adds cedar and spice. Consistent 97-98 point scores in recent vintages.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Troplong Mondot

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Chateau Troplong MondotBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still Red

From Saint-Emilion's highest plateau (over 100m), Troplong Mondot produces Merlot-dominant blends of great richness and depth. Thick and concentrated without being heavy; dark plum, coffee and stone character with remarkable ageing potential.

Cuvée page →

Chateau de la Riviere

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Chateau de la RiviereBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The flagship Fronsac estate red from 60 hectares on clay-limestone soils above the Dordogne. Merlot-led and supple with dark chocolate and ripe cherry; aged in spectacular 16th-century underground cellars at constant 13 degrees.

Cuvée page →

Château Larose-Trintaudon

3.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Château Larose-TrintaudonBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The largest single vineyard in the Médoc produces an consistently approachable, fruit-forward Haut-Médoc with a well-balanced structure and excellent everyday drinkability at an accessible price.

Cuvée page →

Château de Camensac

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Savoury

Château de CamensacBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

A revived Fifth Growth finding its stride under the Merlaut family: Cabernet-forward Haut-Médoc structure with dark fruit, cedar and characteristic menthol freshness from deep gravel soils near Saint-Julien.

Cuvée page →

Croix Canon

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Elegant

Chateau CanonBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

Second wine of Chateau Canon (formerly Clos Canon, rebranded Croix Canon from the 2012 vintage). Elegant, medium-bodied with mint, cherry and floral character; structured enough for 10+ years but designed for earlier pleasure.

Cuvée page →

Echo de Lynch-Bages

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau Lynch-BagesBordeaux$$$$$$$$Still Red

Second wine of Lynch-Bages since 2007 (previously sold as Chateau Haut-Bages Averous). Higher Merlot component than the grand vin provides accessible fruit and earlier drinking; retains characteristic Lynch-Bages tobacco and cassis signature.

Cuvée page →

Fleur de Clinet

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau ClinetBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

The accessible second wine of Clinet from estate vines, made to reveal its qualities early. Smooth-textured, fruit-driven Pomerol Merlot with approachable tannins and genuine ageing potential without requiring long cellaring.

Cuvée page →

L'Héritage de Chasse-Spleen

3.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing

Château Chasse-SpleenBordeaux€€€€€€Still Red

The Haut-Médoc AOC second-level wine of Chasse-Spleen, made from satellite vineyard parcels. Easy-drinking, herb-inflected red fruit for everyday occasions.

Cuvée page →

L'Oratoire de Chasse-Spleen

3.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Fruity

Château Chasse-SpleenBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The approachable second wine of Chasse-Spleen from the Moulis AOC: Merlot-driven, with enticing red fruit and soft tannins, ready to enjoy without extended cellaring.

Cuvée page →

La Chapelle de La Mission Haut-Brion

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Savoury · Elegant

Chateau La Mission Haut-BrionBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still Red

The second wine of La Mission Haut-Brion, incorporating grapes from younger vines and since 2006 from the former La Tour Haut-Brion vineyard. Shares the Talence gravel terroir; accessible dark fruit with cedar and spice, drinkable earlier than the grand vin.

Cuvée page →

La Closerie de Camensac

3.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing

Château de CamensacBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The second wine of Château Camensac, prioritising freshness and supple tannins from younger vine selections. Light and immediately appealing with characteristic Haut-Médoc menthol freshness.

Cuvée page →

La Croix Ducru-Beaucaillou

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Bold

Château Ducru-BeaucaillouBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

Powerful and silky with aromatic richness; introduces the Borie house style at an accessible level with plush berry fruit and balanced oak spice.

Cuvée page →

La Demoiselle de Sociando-Mallet

3.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Château Sociando-MalletBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The Merlot-dominant second wine of Sociando-Mallet, offering earlier approachability with a more typically Médoc fruit profile than the austere and structured grand vin.

Cuvée page →

La Gravette de Certan

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Vieux Chateau CertanBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

The accessible, Merlot-dominant second selection from Vieux Chateau Certan, made from younger vines and less-selected barrels. Forward, fruit-driven, and approachable early but retaining the estate's hallmark iron-mineral signature.

Cuvée page →

La Réserve de Léoville Barton

3.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Château Léoville BartonBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

An elegant and approachable expression of Saint-Julien from younger vines, with a higher Merlot proportion giving early accessibility and soft ripe fruit.

Cuvée page →

Le Carillon de l'Angelus

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Fruity

Chateau AngelusBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

The second wine of Angelus, Merlot-dominant with a forward, polished character. Bright red fruit, soft spice and floral tones make it approachable younger than the grand vin while retaining the estate's Merlot generosity.

Cuvée page →

Le Clarence de Haut-Brion

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Savoury

Chateau Haut-BrionBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still Red

The second red of Haut-Brion, renamed from Bahans Haut-Brion in 2007. Merlot-dominated in most vintages with the same Pessac terroir as the grand vin, but more approachable in youth, showing red plum, blueberry, and mineral freshness.

Cuvée page →

Le Clementin du Pape Clement Rouge

3.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau Pape ClementBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The second red wine of Pape Clement since 1986. Merlot-dominant for approachability; round red cherry and currant fruit with cedar and soft spice notes. Designed for earlier drinking than the grand vin while reflecting the same Pessac terroir.

Cuvée page →

Le Pauillac de Chateau Latour

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau LatourBordeaux$$$$$$$$Still Red

Third wine of Latour produced since 1989. Merlot-dominant and made primarily from young vines; developed for the restaurant market as a generously fruity, earlier-drinking Pauillac.

Cuvée page →

Le Petit Cheval

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Easygoing

Chateau Cheval BlancBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still Red

The second wine of Cheval Blanc: same terroir and typicity as the grand vin but in a more accessible, forward style. Charming black-fruit and cedar profile, silky tannins, and an earlier drinking window.

Cuvée page →

Le Petit Haut Lafitte Rouge

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Savoury

Chateau Smith Haut LafitteBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

The second red wine of Smith Haut Lafitte, sharing the estate's Martillac gravel terroir. A Cabernet-Sauvignon-led blend with cassis, fresh herbs, and pencil-shaving notes in an approachable medium-bodied style suitable for earlier drinking.

Cuvée page →

Le Petit Lion du Marquis de Las Cases

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Elegant

Château Léoville-Las CasesBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

The official second wine of Léoville-Las Cases since 2007, Merlot-dominant for earlier accessibility while retaining the Saint-Julien precision of the parent estate.

Le Pin

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Le PinBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Micro-estate garagiste Pomerol with supremely hedonistic Merlot character: mocha-laced plum and violet aromatics, a voluptuous, fleshy palate, and remarkable concentration from just 400-600 cases per year.

Cuvée page →

Les Hauts de Pontet-Canet

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau Pontet-CanetBordeaux$$$$$$$$Still Red

Second wine of Pontet-Canet since 1982. Produced in limited quantities as Alfred Tesseron prefers lower yields and declassification over large second-wine production; earlier-drinking, approachable Pauillac from biodynamic-certified vineyards.

Cuvée page →

Les Hauts de Trintaudon

3.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Château Larose-TrintaudonBordeaux€€€€€€Still Red

The accessible second wine of Larose-Trintaudon, sourced from the same vines and terroir as the grand vin but crafted for early approachability and everyday enjoyment.

Mondot

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau Troplong MondotBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

Second wine of Troplong Mondot, Merlot-dominant with emphasis on freshness and early drinkability. A juicy, earthy black cherry and spring flower profile with wet-stone limestone terroir character; approachable and unpretentious.

Cuvée page →

Petit Becot

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Fruity

Chateau Beau-Sejour BecotBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The renamed second wine of Beau-Sejour Becot (formerly Tournelle de Beau-Sejour Becot), renamed Petit Becot in 2012. Merlot-dominant with fresh red cherry character and gentle tannin; designed for early drinking pleasure.

Petrus

5.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

PetrusBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

100% Merlot from 11.4 ha of blue clay at Pomerol's centre. Silk-and-velvet texture with truffle, chocolate, and dark cherry at its core; structurally imposing yet hedonistic, with a finish that stretches for minutes.

Cuvée page →

Prieur de Meyney

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau MeyneyBordeaux$$$$$$$Still Red

Second wine of Chateau Meyney since 1979, named for the estate's 17th-century priory origins. Produced from younger vines (approximately 35-40 years) using minimal new oak to preserve fresh fruit character; a reliable everyday Saint-Estephe.

A fine twenty-year tawny with honeyed dried fruit, nuts and a long candied-citrus finish.

Sweet · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Savoury

★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Festive

Williams & HumbertJerez & Sherry€€€€€€Fortified Sherry

A classic medium sherry, the style that made Williams & Humbert famous. Off-dry with dried apricot, almond toffee and gentle spice from a blend of Amontillado, Oloroso and PX soleras.

Cuvée page →

East India Solera Cream

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Medium Sweet · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Festive · Elegant

Emilio LustauJerez & Sherry€€€€€€€Fortified Sherry

Lustau's East India Solera evokes the historical 'Manila Galleon' practice of shipping sherry in barrels across the tropics to India, which accelerated ageing. Rich, sweet and complex with raisins, toffee and vanilla.

Cuvée page →

Emperatriz Eugenia Oloroso VORS

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Emilio LustauJerez & Sherry€€€€€€€€Fortified Sherry

VORS Oloroso of extraordinary age and depth. Old leather, tobacco, dark coffee and dried orange peel with a very long dry and warming finish.

Cuvée page →

Faraón Oloroso

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Bodegas Hidalgo La GitanaJerez & Sherry€€€€€€€€Fortified Sherry

VORS Oloroso of 30-plus years average age from Sanlúcar; intensely concentrated with dark dried-fruit, coffee and rancio depth alongside the characteristic saline note of the appellation.

Domaine de la MordoreeRhone Valley€€€€€€€€Still Red

The prestige Chateauneuf from La Mordoree; dark, rich, and muscular with Mourvedre backbone and a silky tannic finish. Consistently one of the appellation's finest.

Fresh, aromatic white Lirac from La Mordoree; multi-variety Grenache Blanc-led blend with white blossom, peach, and herb character.

Domaine du CayronRhone Valley€€€€€€€Still Red

★★★★★★★★★★

Baglio di PianettoSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Ramione blends the dark fruit warmth of Nero d'Avola with the softening effect of Merlot at 900 metres above sea level: the result is a polished, smooth Sicilian blend with surprising freshness from altitude.

Baglio di Pianetto Viafrancia

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Savoury

Baglio di PianettoSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Baglio di Pianetto's high-altitude Syrah shows how the variety performs at 900 metres: the elevation preserves aromatic freshness and pepper-spice that is often lost at lower-altitude Sicilian warmth.

Cusumano's Nero d'Avola and Syrah blend from Sicilian estate vineyards: the pepper-spice of Syrah amplifies Nero d'Avola's dark fruit and the barrique treatment adds structure and warmth.

Cuvée page →

Cusumano Jale

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Bold

CusumanoSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Cusumano's premium Chardonnay, Jale, shows the full-sun ripeness of Sicilian Chardonnay through barrique treatment: rich, creamy and tropical with hazelnut-vanilla depth.

Cuvée page →

Cusumano Sagana

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Contemplative

CusumanoSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Sagana is Cusumano's prestige single-vineyard Nero d'Avola from Riesi in central Sicily: opulent, concentrated and barrique-treated to produce a richly chocolate-dark wine of considerable structure.

Cuvée page →

Donnafugata Ben Rye Passito di Pantelleria

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Festive · Romantic

Donnafugata PantelleriaSicily€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Ben Rye (Arabic for 'son of the wind') is Italy's most celebrated Passito di Pantelleria: sun-dried Zibibbo from the alberello pantesco vines on the volcanic island, achieving a concentration and aromatic complexity that has defined the appellation's international reputation.

Cuvée page →

Donnafugata Kabir Moscato di Pantelleria

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Refreshing · Festive

Donnafugata PantelleriaSicily€€€€€€€Dessert Late Harvest

Kabir is Donnafugata's lighter Pantelleria sweet wine: fresher and less concentrated than Ben Rye, from Zibibbo grapes dried for a shorter period, producing a floral, juicy dessert wine of great charm.

Cuvée page →

Donnafugata Lighea

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Refreshing · Fruity

Donnafugata PantelleriaSicily€€€€€€Still White

Lighea is Donnafugata's dry-to-off-dry Zibibbo table wine from Pantelleria: fresh, aromatic, with the grape's characteristic muscat-like floral and apricot character in a lighter, immediately approachable style.

Cuvée page →

Donnafugata Mille e Una Notte

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

DonnafugataSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Donnafugata's flagship red, Mille e Una Notte blends Nero d'Avola with Petit Verdot and Syrah from the Contessa Entellina estate: concentrated, structured and age-worthy with exceptional depth.

Cuvée page →

Donnafugata Sherazade

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

DonnafugataSicily€€€€€€Still Red

Donnafugata's entry-level Nero d'Avola offers the grape's warm, plum-cherry character with the smooth, approachable style the estate is known for at this price point.

Cuvée page →

Donnafugata Tancredi

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Bold

DonnafugataSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Tancredi blends the warmth of Nero d'Avola with Cabernet Sauvignon's structural backbone, producing a Sicilian-Bordeaux hybrid with cedar-cedar depth and concentrated fruit aged gracefully in French barriques.

Cuvée page →

Duca di Salaparuta Bianca di Valguarnera

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Savoury

Duca di Salaparuta (Corvo)Sicily€€€€€€€Still White

Bianca di Valguarnera is Sicily's most celebrated Inzolia: Duca di Salaparuta's partial barrique treatment of the native grape produces a rich, nutty, mineral white with the depth to age several years.

Cuvée page →

Duca di Salaparuta Duca Enrico

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Duca di Salaparuta (Corvo)Sicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Duca Enrico was Sicily's first landmark 100% Nero d'Avola: barrique-aged, structured and concentrated, it defined the modern Sicilian red wine archetype and remains a pillar of the island's wine history.

Cuvée page →

Feudi del Pisciotto Versace Nero d'Avola

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Bold

Feudi del PisciottoSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

The Versace-labelled Nero d'Avola from Feudi del Pisciotto is part of the estate's designer fashion-house label series; the wine is a full, smooth and polished Nero d'Avola of immediate approachability.

Cuvée page →

Feudo Montoni Lagnusa

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Rustic · Bold

Feudo MontoniSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

The more approachable Feudo Montoni Nero d'Avola; less austere than Vrucara, with a cherry-herb character and the estate's distinctive high-altitude mineral warmth.

Cuvée page →

Firriato Camelot

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Bold

FirriatoSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Firriato's Bordeaux-style blend from the Trapani hinterland: Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot grown in Sicily's sun produce a richly ripe interpretation of the classic Bordeaux blend with a warm cedar-tobacco finish.

Cuvée page →

Firriato Chiaramonte Nero d'Avola

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

FirriatoSicily€€€€€€Still Red

Firriato's entry-level Nero d'Avola under the Chiaramonte label is an accessible, fruit-forward expression of the variety: warm, rounded and designed for everyday enjoyment.

Cuvée page →

Florio Marsala Vergine Riserva

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Cantine FlorioSicily€€€€€€€Fortified Marsala

Florio's Marsala Vergine Riserva is a benchmark for the driest and most complex Marsala style: fortified without added must, aged minimum 10 years in large oak in the tuff-stone cellars, developing deep rancio oxidative complexity.

Cuvée page →

Florio Morsi di Luce Passito di Pantelleria

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Festive · Romantic

Cantine FlorioSicily€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Morsi di Luce (bites of light) is Florio's Passito di Pantelleria: sun-dried Zibibbo grapes from Pantelleria producing a sweet, honeyed wine with apricot and orange blossom aromatics.

Cuvée page →

Florio Targa Riserva 1840

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Medium Sweet · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Festive · Contemplative

Cantine FlorioSicily€€€€€€€Fortified Marsala

The Targa Riserva 1840 is Florio's ambassador wine: a Marsala Superiore Riserva of medium-sweet richness, aged 5-plus years in the historic cellars, balancing caramelised dried-fruit complexity with the freshness of Grillo.

Cuvée page →

Florio Terre Arse Marsala Vergine

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Savoury

Cantine FlorioSicily€€€€€€€€Fortified Marsala

Terre Arse (burnt lands - a reference to Marsala's sun-scorched landscape) is Florio's dry-style Marsala Vergine mid-tier: oxidative, nutty and almond-driven, without the full intensity of the Riserva.

Cuvée page →

Gulfi Nerobaronj

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Savoury

GulfiSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

The Baronj contrada produces a more herbal-mineral Nero d'Avola than Bufaleffj, with a finer-grained tannic structure and exceptional length.

Cuvée page →

Gulfi Nerobufaleffj

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Contemplative

GulfiSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

The Bufaleffj contrada produces Gulfi's most powerful Nero d'Avola: dense, iron-mineral, dark-fruited and structured with high tannins needing several years to integrate.

Cuvée page →

Gulfi Nerojbleo

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Rustic

GulfiSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Gulfi's entry-level single-contrada Nero d'Avola from the Ibleo plateau: concentrated dark fruit, carob and licorice with firm tannin and a warm volcanic-clay mineral finish.

Cuvée page →

Gulfi Nerosanlore

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

GulfiSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Sanlorenzo is the most elegant of Gulfi's quartet: the highest-altitude contrada delivers a more refined palate structure with exceptional aromatic complexity.

Cuvée page →

Hauner Malvasia delle Lipari Naturale

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Medium Sweet · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Refreshing · Festive

Hauner (Malvasia delle Lipari)Sicily€€€€€€€Dessert Late Harvest

The Naturale style is a lighter, fresher take on Malvasia delle Lipari: less concentrated than the Passito but showing the grape's characteristic floral-apricot aromatics and the volcanic salinity of the Aeolian Islands in a more immediately approachable register.

Cuvée page →

Hauner Malvasia delle Lipari Passito

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Festive · Romantic

Hauner (Malvasia delle Lipari)Sicily€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Hauner's Malvasia delle Lipari Passito is the benchmark for this rare Aeolian appellation: sun-dried Malvasia di Lipari producing a wine of extraordinary floral complexity, apricot sweetness and the distinctive volcanic mineral character of the island of Salina.

Cuvée page →

Maggio Vini Nero d'Avola

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Rustic

Maggio ViniSicily€€€€€€Still Red

Maggio Vini's Nero d'Avola from the ultra-sun-drenched Maccari-Noto area offers one of the island's richest and warmest expressions of the variety: full-bodied, carob-dark-fruited with licorice depth.

Cuvée page →

Mandrarossa Nero d'Avola

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Settesoli MandrarossaSicily€€€€€€Still Red

Mandrarossa's varietal Nero d'Avola shows the cooperative's Menfi terroir at its most accessible: cherry-plum, herbal and warm, a reliable everyday Sicilian red with the typical coastal-influence freshness of Menfi.

Cuvée page →

Marco De Bartoli Bukkuram Sole d'Agosto

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Funky

Marco De Bartoli - Bukkuram (Pantelleria)Sicily€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Sole d'Agosto (August sun) is the De Bartoli minimal-intervention take on Passito di Pantelleria: zero-sulphur Zibibbo from the Bukkuram estate, producing an almost overwhelmingly concentrated dried-fruit and honey wine with a volcanic mineral dimension absent from other Pantelleria passiti.

Cuvée page →

Marco De Bartoli Marsala Superiore Riserva 10 Anni

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Medium Sweet · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Festive

Marco De BartoliSicily€€€€€€€€Fortified Marsala

De Bartoli's 10-year Marsala Superiore Riserva is produced with minimal fortification compared to standard Marsala, preserving more natural character from the Grillo grape: the result is lighter in oxidative weight than the Vergine range but more genuinely grape-expressive than industrial Marsala.

Cuvée page →

Marco De Bartoli Marsala Superiore Riserva Oro

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Festive · Easygoing

Marco De BartoliSicily€€€€€€€Fortified Marsala

De Bartoli's Marsala Superiore Riserva Oro is an approachable 5-year expression: lighter oxidative weight than the 10-year Riserva but with the estate's characteristic Grillo purity and a golden-almond character that bridges the aperitivo and dessert register.

Cuvée page →

Marco De Bartoli Vecchio Samperi

5.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Funky

Marco De BartoliSicily€€€€€€€€Fortified Marsala

Vecchio Samperi is Marco De Bartoli's life work and Sicily's most important wine: un-fortified perpetuo Grillo aged in a 5-tier solera at Samperi, achieving Marsala-like depth and complexity entirely through natural oxidation - no fortification spirits added. Among the world's most distinctive and debated fortified-style wines.

Cuvée page →

Marco De Bartoli Vigna La Miccia Marsala Oro

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Marco De BartoliSicily€€€€€€€Fortified Marsala

Vigna La Miccia is De Bartoli's single-vineyard Marsala Superiore Oro: more approachable and lighter in oxidative weight than the Riserva, produced from a specific Grillo plot near Samperi with fresher fruit character.

Cuvée page →

Morgante Don Antonio

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Contemplative

MorganteSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Don Antonio is Morgante's single-vineyard selection: the richest, most concentrated Nero d'Avola from the estate with barrique aging producing a wine of chocolate-dark-fruit depth and silky structure.

Cuvée page →

Morgante Nero d'Avola

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

MorganteSicily€€€€€€Still Red

Morgante's estate Nero d'Avola from the Agrigento interior shows the full-bodied, warm-fruited character of the grape from hot-climate inland soils, with approachable tannin and immediate appeal.

Cuvée page →

Occhipinti Siccagno

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Arianna OcchipintiSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Siccagno is the Sicilian dry-farming term for unirrigated cultivation; Occhipinti's varietal Nero d'Avola is richly dark-fruited with a characteristic iron-mineral finish from Vittoria's ferrous soils.

Cuvée page →

Pellegrino Marsala Superiore Riserva 10 Anni

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Medium Sweet · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Festive

Cantine PellegrinoSicily€€€€€€€Fortified Marsala

Pellegrino's 10-year Marsala Superiore Riserva is a showcase for the family-owned house's century-long expertise: 10 years of barrel ageing develop extraordinary complexity of dried fruit, coffee and roasted nut character.

Cuvée page →

Pellegrino Marsala Vergine Soleras

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Savoury

Cantine PellegrinoSicily€€€€€€€Fortified Marsala

Pellegrino's Vergine Soleras uses a traditional solera system (perpetuo) maintaining wine from multiple decades: a layered, dry Marsala of walnut-and-apricot complexity that reflects the long Pellegrino history.

Cuvée page →

Pellegrino Moscato di Pantelleria

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Refreshing · Festive

Cantine PellegrinoSicily€€€€€€Dessert Late Harvest

The Moscato di Pantelleria is a lighter, fresher sweet wine style compared to the passito: Zibibbo grapes not fully dried produce a wine of floral freshness, bright apricot character and lower alcohol, ideal as an aperitivo or light dessert wine.

Cuvée page →

Pellegrino Nes Passito di Pantelleria

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Festive · Romantic

Cantine PellegrinoSicily€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Nes (shortened from the Pantelleria dialect 'giardino pantesco', an enclosed garden) is Pellegrino's sun-drenched Passito di Pantelleria: concentrated Zibibbo with apricot-honey character and the characteristic volcanic depth of Pantelleria.

Cuvée page →

Planeta Burdese

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

PlanetaSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Burdese is Planeta's prestige Cabernet Sauvignon-Franc blend, named in Sicilian dialect for Bordeaux. Grown at Ulmo in Menfi, the wine achieves the concentration of ripeness that Sicily's sun provides while preserving the variety's structural backbone through altitude and careful vinification.

Cuvée page →

Planeta Chardonnay

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Bold

PlanetaSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Planeta's barrique-aged Chardonnay from Ulmo at Menfi became one of the wines that announced Sicily's quality transformation in the 1990s: rich, buttery, confident, with Sicilian sunshine layered through Burgundian oak treatment.

Cuvée page →

Planeta La Segreta Rosso

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

PlanetaSicily€€€€€€Still Red

La Segreta Rosso is Planeta's multi-varietal entry red: Nero d'Avola blended with Merlot, Syrah and Cabernet Franc for a smooth, approachable and consistent everyday Sicilian red with broad food versatility.

Cuvée page →

Planeta Noto Nero d'Avola

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Rustic

Planeta Buonivini (Noto)Sicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Planeta's Noto DOC Nero d'Avola from the Buonivini contrada captures the south-east of Sicily at its most sun-soaked: rich carob and dark cherry with the warm mineral finish typical of the Noto appellation's limestone soils.

Cuvée page →

Planeta Santa Cecilia

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Contemplative

PlanetaSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Santa Cecilia is Planeta's prestige single-estate Nero d'Avola from Noto: rich, structured and mineral with chocolate-dark-fruit depth and the long finish that defines this appellation.

Cuvée page →

Rapitala Grand Cru

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Bold

Tenuta RapitalaSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Rapitala Grand Cru is the estate's flagship Chardonnay: a full barrique-fermented Sicilian Chardonnay from the Camporeale hillside estate combining tropical fruit richness with a toasty-creamy Burgundian framework.

Cuvée page →

Rapitala Hugonis

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Bold

Tenuta RapitalaSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Hugonis, named for the founding de la Gatinais family, blends Cabernet Sauvignon's structure with Nero d'Avola's Sicilian warmth in a wine that reflects the estate's Franco-Sicilian origins.

Cuvée page →

Serragghia Passito di Pantelleria

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Funky

Serragghia (Pantelleria)Sicily€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Gabrio Bini's Serragghia is the cult natural passito of Pantelleria: zero-sulphur, zero additions, biodynamic Zibibbo on volcanic pumice soils producing a wine of wild, unpredictable character and extraordinary mineral depth - the most challenging and most sought-after natural sweet wine in Sicily.

Cuvée page →

Tasca d'Almerita Capofaro Malvasia delle Lipari

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Festive · Romantic

Tasca d'Almerita Capofaro (Salina)Sicily€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Capofaro's Malvasia delle Lipari Passito is produced at one of Italy's most dramatically situated wineries, with terrace vines overlooking Stromboli. The Tasca d'Almerita winemaking precision elevates the passito style with clean, precise floral-honeyed concentration and the distinctive volcanic mineral signature of Salina.

Cuvée page →

Tasca d'Almerita Chardonnay

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Bold

Tasca d'AlmeritaSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Tasca's Chardonnay from the high Regaleali plateau shows what Sicilian sun and altitude do to Chardonnay: richer and more tropical than Burgundy but retaining the Regaleali site's mineral backbone.

Cuvée page →

Tasca d'Almerita Regaleali Rosso

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Tasca d'AlmeritaSicily€€€€€€Still Red

Regaleali Rosso has been Tasca's everyday red since 1968: a multi-varietal native blend of Nero d'Avola, Perricone and Nerello Mascalese showing the breadth of the Regaleali plateau's terroir.

Cuvée page →

Tenuta Gatti Nero d'Avola

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Rustic · Savoury

Tenuta GattiSicily€€€€€€Still Red

Tenuta Gatti's Nero d'Avola from the Nebrodi slopes in the Messina province is unusually mineral and herbal compared to southern Sicilian examples: the altitude and Nebrodi scrubland influence give a cooler, more aromatic character to this northerly Nero d'Avola.

Valle dell'AcateSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Valle dell'Acate's varietal Nero d'Avola shows the grape's fuller, warmer side: ripe dark fruit with vanilla-spice from French oak, round tannins and a warming Sicilian sun-drenched finish.

Zisola (Mazzei)Sicily€€€€€€€Still Red

The estate red blends Nero d'Avola's dark fruit with Syrah's peppery spice, delivering a richer, more internationally styled Sicilian red with smooth tannin and warmth.

Doppiozeta is Zisola's flagship Nero d'Avola from Noto DOC: Mazzei's Tuscan winemaking precision applied to Sicilian Nero d'Avola produces a wine of elegant structure and tobacco-mineral depth.

Adami dei Casel Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

AdamiVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The entry-level Extra Dry from Adami. Dei Casel (meaning 'of the hamlet') has been the house's everyday accessible label since the early decades of production. Consistent, clean and approachable.

Cuvée page →

Bele Casel Asolo Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Bele CaselVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Asolo Prosecco DOCG is produced on the south-facing slopes of the Asolo hills, southwest of Valdobbiadene. Bele Casel uses indigenous Perera and Verdiso alongside Glera for a more complex aromatic profile than monovarietal Prosecco.

Cuvée page →

Bisol Cartizze Valdobbiadene Superiore di Cartizze

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Festive · Elegant

Bisol 1542Veneto€€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Rich and aromatic expression from the 107-ha Cartizze grand cru hill. Bisol's long tenure on the hill and multi-generational vine stock give this off-dry sparkler a layered complexity unusual in Prosecco.

Cuvée page →

Bisol Jeio Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Bisol 1542Veneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Jeio is the accessible everyday Brut tier from Bisol 1542, positioned below the premium Crede multi-varietal and the Rive expressions. The Glera-dominant blend with minor Verdiso and Bianchetta adds a touch of aromatic complexity to a clean, food-friendly Brut.

Cuvée page →

Bisol Relio Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Bisol 1542Veneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Extra Dry in Prosecco parlance is slightly sweeter (12-17 g/l residual sugar) than Brut. Relio is a soft, fruit-forward style with rounded acidity, suited to lighter food pairings and aperitivo moments.

Cuvée page →

Bortolomiol Bandarossa Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Fruity · Easygoing

BortolomiolVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Bandarossa (red ribbon) is a fruitier, more aromatic Extra Dry than Senior. Designed for pairing with fruit-forward desserts and aromatic preparations, it expresses the warmer side of Valdobbiadene's Glera.

Cuvée page →

Bortolomiol Senior Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

BortolomiolVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The flagship Extra Dry from Bortolomiol, founded in 1949 by Giuliano Bortolomiol. Named 'Senior' in tribute to the founder, this is a classic off-dry Valdobbiadene DOCG with the balance of sweetness and freshness that made the Extra Dry category popular.

Cuvée page →

Cantina di Custoza Bianco di Custoza DOC

3.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing

Cantina di CustozaVeneto€€€€€€Still White

The cooperative's flagship Bianco di Custoza is the appellation's most accessible, high-volume expression. Consistent, clean and approachable, it represents good-value Custoza DOC at the walk-in tourist tier. The five-variety blend from cooperative members' morainic vineyards gives a typical zonal character.

Cuvée page →

Cantina di Custoza La Selva Bianco di Custoza DOC

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium acidity · Elegant · Savoury

Cantina di CustozaVeneto€€€€€€Still White

La Selva is the cooperative's premium single-vineyard selection from older vines in the Selva parcel. Partial oak fermentation adds texture and complexity beyond the standard cooperative Custoza, demonstrating the appellation's premium potential.

Cuvée page →

Cavalchina Amedeo Bianco di Custoza DOC

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium acidity · Elegant · Savoury

CavalchinaVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

Amedeo is Cavalchina's premium selection, using partial oak fermentation to add complexity and longevity to the Bianco di Custoza DOC framework. More structured and richer than the standard Custoza, with the oak well integrated.

Cuvée page →

Col Vetoraz Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Fruity

Col Vetoraz SpumantiVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The Extra Dry expression from Col Vetoraz's elevated hilltop estate. Off-dry with gentle floral sweetness; the altitude of the estate prevents the Extra Dry from tipping into overripe territory, maintaining freshness.

Cuvée page →

Col Vetoraz Valdobbiadene Superiore di Cartizze

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Festive · Elegant

Col Vetoraz SpumantiVeneto€€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Col Vetoraz is uniquely positioned above Santo Stefano at the edge of the Cartizze hill, giving its Cartizze expression exceptional altitude and south-facing exposure. The result is an opulent, aromatic Cartizze with great aromatic intensity and length.

Cuvée page →

Corte Gardoni Bianco di Custoza DOC

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Corte GardoniVeneto€€€€€€Still White

The Piccoli family's Bianco di Custoza from the morainic rim at Valeggio sul Mincio. The glacially deposited soils on the southern slope of the Lake Garda moraine produce a fresh, food-friendly white with characteristic saline mineral character.

Cuvée page →

Dal Forno Romano Recioto della Valpolicella

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Romantic · Contemplative

Dal Forno RomanoVeneto€€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Dal Forno's sweet Recioto applies the same barrique-and-concentration philosophy: deep, almost port-like sweetness balanced by the estate's signature tannin structure.

Cuvée page →

Foss Marai Drusian Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Foss MaraiVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Drusian is the flagship Extra Dry from Foss Marai, named after the locality of the Guia vineyards. Softer and more aromatic than the Brut, with white peach and acacia blossom in an off-dry, easy-drinking style.

Cuvée page →

Giuseppe Quintarelli Recioto della Valpolicella Classico

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Romantic

Giuseppe QuintarelliVeneto€€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

The sweet passito counterpart to Quintarelli's Amarone: residual sugar balanced by firm tannins and high acidity producing a luscious yet structured dessert red with decades-long ageing potential.

Cuvée page →

Gorgo Merlot Garda DOC

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

GorgoVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

Gorgo's Garda DOC Merlot provides a red wine counterpart to the estate's primary Bianco di Custoza focus. Lake Garda's moderating effect on the climate produces a well-rounded Merlot with soft tannins and ripe fruit.

Cuvée page →

Guerrieri Rizzardi Bardolino Classico

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Guerrieri RizzardiVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

A classic light-bodied Bardolino Classico from the Lake Garda shoreline estates. The Classico denomination requires production from the original Bardolino historic core. Guerrieri Rizzardi, as one of the oldest estates in the zone, produces a benchmark expression of the pale ruby, cherry-scented style.

Cuvée page →

Italo Cescon Il Tralcetto Pinot Grigio delle Venezie

3.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Italo CesconVeneto€€€€€€Still White

The volume accessible white from Italo Cescon. Pinot Grigio delle Venezie DOC is the broad appellation for Pinot Grigio from the Tre Venezie; the Cescon version is produced from Piave valley floor vineyards and represents the commercial, food-versatile tier of the estate alongside the character-driven Raboso.

Cuvée page →

Italo Cescon Merlot Piave DOC

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Italo CesconVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

The Piave plain Merlot DOC from Italo Cescon is a classic Trevisan everyday red. Merlot on the alluvial Piave soils produces a plum-forward, medium-bodied wine with soft tannins suited to simple food pairings.

Cuvée page →

Lorenzo Begali Recioto della Valpolicella Classico

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Romantic · Contemplative

Lorenzo BegaliVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Begali's Recioto is made from the same Ca' Bianca old-vine fruit as the Amarone, with the fermentation arrested before dryness; the result is a luscious, chocolate-cherry sweet red with firm tannic structure.

Cuvée page →

Maculan Dindarello Breganze

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Festive · Romantic

MaculanVeneto€€€€€€€Dessert Late Harvest

Dindarello is Maculan's late-harvest Moscato, a lighter and more delicate counterpart to the intense Torcolato and Acininobili. From small Moscato (Muscat blanc a petits grains) grapes left on the vine to develop extra ripeness, it captures the variety's natural floral sweetness without botrytis intervention.

Cuvée page →

Maculan Pino and Toi Breganze Bianco

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

MaculanVeneto€€€€€€Still White

Pino and Toi (Pine and You in Veneto dialect) is Maculan's accessible dry white, a refreshing blend of the two Pinots and Tocai for everyday drinking. It represents the lighter, approachable side of the Breganze DOC in contrast to the estate's intense dessert wines.

Cuvée page →

Masi Campofiorin Rosso del Veronese

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Masi AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Campofiorin pioneered the ripasso technique in 1964, passing fresh Valpolicella wine over dried Amarone grape skins to extract colour, richness and complexity. A seminal wine that created an entire style category.

Cuvée page →

Ruggeri Cartizze Valdobbiadene Superiore di Cartizze

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Festive · Elegant

Ruggeri and C.Veneto€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Ruggeri's Cartizze expression from the storied 107-hectare grand cru hill. Off-dry and aromatic with richer fruit and honey than the standard Valdobbiadene range, showing the concentration that the south-facing Cartizze slopes produce.

Cuvée page →

Ruggeri Giustino B Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Ruggeri and C.Veneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The historical benchmark of Ruggeri, first produced when the house was founded in 1950. Giustino B is named after Giustino Bisol, who co-founded the house; this Extra Dry expression became one of the most recognised Prosecco Superiore labels in the zone.

Cuvée page →

Ruggeri Oro Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing

Ruggeri and C.Veneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Ruggeri Oro is the accessible volume entry-level Extra Dry, designed for everyday aperitivo occasions. Consistent, clean and fruit-forward with the classic Prosecco Superiore profile.

Cuvée page →

Serafini and Vidotto Bollicine di Prosecco

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing

Serafini and VidottoVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The Prosecco DOC entry point from the Montello red-wine estate. Serafini and Vidotto produces Bollicine di Prosecco from Treviso-zone Glera as an accessible sparkling alongside the estate's main red wine mission.

Cuvée page →

Tommasi Recioto della Valpolicella Classico

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Romantic · Festive

Tommasi ViticoltoriVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Tommasi's Recioto Classico is made from the highest-selected Corvina and Corvinone bunches from the family's San Pietro in Cariano vineyards; chocolate-cherry and raisin sweetness balanced by traditional cask structure.

Cuvée page →

Villa Sandi Cartizze Valdobbiadene Superiore di Cartizze Dry

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Medium Sweet · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Festive · Elegant

Villa SandiVeneto€€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Villa Sandi's Cartizze Dry is at the sweeter end of the Cartizze spectrum (17-32 g/l residual sugar). The grand cru hill's concentration of sunny exposure and south-facing slopes amplifies fruit ripeness into ripe peach and mango territory.

Cuvée page →

Villa Sandi Il Fresco Prosecco DOC Treviso Brut

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Villa SandiVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Villa Sandi's volume Prosecco DOC expression. Il Fresco is the globally distributed everyday label from the Palladian villa estate in Crocetta del Montello; consistent, approachable and affordable at the DOC level.
